name: armorcodex
description: ArmorIQ intent-based security enforcement for Codex: Bash command guardrails with intent verification, optional CSRG cryptographic proofs, and audit logging. Codex hooks currently fire for Bash, apply_patch, and MCP tool calls; non-Bash file/web activity may need supplemental controls. See CODEX_HARNESS_LIMITATIONS.md.

interface.shortDescription: Intent-based security policy and audit for Codex.
interface.longDescription: ArmorIQ intent-based security enforcement for OpenAI Codex. Treat as a strong Bash guardrail and audit layer, not a complete boundary for every Codex capability. Codex hooks currently emit Bash, apply_patch, and MCP tool calls. ArmorCodex provides plan registration through MCP, intent-plan matching, permission gating, and post-run audit on those tools. Non-Bash activity (file edits, web search, app connectors) is gated where Codex emits hook events.
defaultPrompt: Show me what security rules are protecting this project.
Block any commands that fetch URLs or exfiltrate data.
Walk me through your plan before running anything.

api_key.title: ArmorIQ API Key
api_key.description: Your ArmorIQ API key (get one at https://armoriq.ai). Leave blank to run in local-only mode without backend audit/intent.

mode.title: Enforcement Mode
mode.description: enforce = block on policy/intent failures (recommended). monitor = log only, never block.

intent_required.title: Require Intent Plan
intent_required.description: When true, every Bash command must be backed by a registered intent plan (Codex hooks currently only intercept Bash). Disable for advisory-only use.

crypto_policy_enabled.title: Enable Crypto Policy Binding
crypto_policy_enabled.description: Bind policy rules to a Merkle tree so post-issuance tampering is detected.

use_production.title: Use Production Endpoints
use_production.description: When true, talks to ArmorIQ production. When false, expects a local backend on 127.0.0.1.
